html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Six — Part Three

The Book of Chanokh

CHANOKH

The Epistle of Chanokh — DSS Attested

Chapters 56 through 73

Walk in Righteousness — The Woes Against the Sinners and the Mighty — The Heavenly Books Are Opened — The Birth of Noakh — I Will Seat Each on the Throne of His Honor

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Dead Sea Scrolls — 4QEng (4Q212) — Epistle of Chanokh — Aramaic Primary

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE EPISTLE OF CHANOKH — CHAPTERS 56 THROUGH 73

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 56 The Exhortation to Metushelakh — Walk in Righteousness

1 And now, my son Metushelakh, call to me all your brothers and gather to me all the sons of your mother, and the word of my voice will speak to them, that they may see everything which will come upon them to the end.

2 And he called and gathered the sons of his sons to himself.

3 And I spoke to all the children of righteousness and said: Hear, you sons of Chanokh, all the words of your father, and hearken aright to the voice of my mouth — for I exhort you and say unto you, beloved: Love uprightness and walk therein.

4 And draw not nigh to uprightness with a double heart, and associate not with those of a double heart, but walk in righteousness, my sons. And it shall guide you on good paths, and righteousness shall be your companion.

5 For I know that violence must increase on the earth, and a great chastisement be executed on the earth, and all unrighteousness come to an end. Yea, it shall be cut off from its roots, and its whole structure be destroyed.

6 And unrighteousness shall again be consummated on the earth, and all the deeds of unrighteousness and of violence and transgression shall prevail in a twofold degree.

7 And when sin and unrighteousness and blasphemy and violence in all kinds of deeds increase, and apostasy and transgression and uncleanness increase, a great chastisement shall come from heaven upon all these, and the holy Lord will come forth with wrath and chastisement to execute judgment on earth.

8 In those days violence shall be cut off from its roots, and the roots of unrighteousness together with deceit, and they shall be destroyed from under heaven.

9 And all the idols of the heathen shall be abandoned, and the temples burned with fire, and they shall remove them from the whole earth, and they shall be cast into the judgment of fire, and shall perish in wrath and in grievous judgment forever.

10 And the righteous shall arise from their sleep, and wisdom shall arise and be given unto them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves substantial portions of Chapter 91 in Aramaic. The Epistle of Chanokh — Chapters 91-108 — is the section of the book most extensively confirmed in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 91 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 91:3 — hear, you sons of Chanokh, all the words of your father. The Epistle of Chanokh is the direct address from Chanokh to his descendants — transmitted through Metushelakh — that forms the final section of the book. Its structure mirrors Moshe's final addresses in Devarim and Yaakov's blessing in Bereshit 49. The patriarch at the end of his earthly time gathers his descendants and transmits the covenant knowledge he has received. The transmission is both practical — walk in righteousness, do not associate with the double-hearted — and eschatological: the history of violence will climax in a great judgment that destroys unrighteousness and raises the righteous from their sleep.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 57 The Book of Chanokh Written for Future Generations

1 The book written by Chanokh — Chanokh indeed wrote this complete doctrine of wisdom, which is praised of all men and a judge of all the earth — for all my children who shall dwell on the earth and for the future generations who shall observe uprightness and peace.

2 Let not your spirit be troubled on account of the times, for the Holy and Great One has appointed days for all things.

3 And the righteous one shall arise from sleep, shall arise and walk in the paths of righteousness, and all his path and conversation shall be in eternal goodness and grace.

4 He will be gracious to the righteous and give him eternal uprightness, and he will give him power so that he shall be endowed with goodness and righteousness, and he shall walk in eternal light.

5 And sin shall perish in darkness forever, and shall no more be seen from that day forevermore.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 92 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 92 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 92:1 — the book written by Chanokh, for all my children who shall dwell on the earth and for the future generations who shall observe uprightness and peace. The Epistle of Chanokh contains the book's own statement of its intended audience: all children on earth, all future generations who observe uprightness. The Book of Chanokh is not addressed to a single generation or community. Its scope is universal and trans-generational. The Qumran community's preservation of it in multiple manuscript copies is their practical response to this universal address — they were the current link in the transmission chain to all future generations.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 58 The Apocalypse of Weeks — The First Seven Weeks of History

1 And after that Chanokh both gave and began to recount from the books. And Chanokh said: Concerning the children of righteousness and concerning the elect of the world, and concerning the plant of uprightness, I will speak these things, yea, I Chanokh will declare them unto you, my sons.

2 I was born the seventh in the first week, while judgment and righteousness still endured.

3 And after me there shall arise in the second week great wickedness, and deceit shall have sprung up, and in it there shall be the first end. And in it a man shall be saved, and after it is ended unrighteousness shall grow up, and a law shall be made for the sinners.

4 And after that in the third week at its close a man shall be elected as the plant of righteous judgment, and his posterity shall become the plant of righteousness forevermore.

5 And after that in the fourth week, at its close, visions of the holy and righteous shall be seen, and a law for all generations and an enclosure shall be made for them.

6 And after that in the fifth week, at its close, the house of glory and dominion shall be built forever.

7 And after that in the sixth week all who live in it shall be blinded, and the hearts of all of them shall godlessly forsake wisdom. And in it a man shall ascend, and at its close the house of dominion shall be burnt with fire, and the whole race of the chosen root shall be dispersed.

8 And after that in the seventh week shall an apostate generation arise, and many shall be its deeds, and all its deeds shall be apostate. And at its close shall be elected the elect righteous of the eternal plant of righteousness, to receive sevenfold instruction concerning all his creation.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves substantial portions of Chapter 93 in Aramaic. The Apocalypse of Weeks is one of the most extensively attested passages in the DSS Chanokh corpus.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 93 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Apocalypse of Weeks divides all of history into ten weeks — ten epochs — each representing a major transition in the covenant history of humanity. Chanokh identifies himself as the seventh generation born in the first week — Bereshit 5 confirms he is the seventh from Adam. The second week is the Flood — a man saved, Noakh. The third week is Avraham — the plant of righteous judgment. The fourth week is the giving of the Torah — the law for all generations. The fifth week is the building of the First Temple — the house of glory and dominion. The sixth week ends with the burning of the Temple and the dispersion — the Babylonian exile. The seventh week — Chanokh's own time in the eschatological future — produces an apostate generation followed by the election of the righteous remnant who receive sevenfold instruction. Chapters 91:12-17 continue this schema through the tenth week.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 59 The Woes Against the Sinners — The Warning to the Righteous

1 And now I say unto you, my sons, love righteousness and walk therein — for the paths of righteousness are worthy of acceptation, but the paths of unrighteousness shall suddenly be destroyed and vanish.

2 And to certain men of a generation shall the paths of violence and of death be revealed, and they shall hold themselves afar from them, and shall not follow them.

3 And now I say unto you the righteous: Walk not in the paths of wickedness, nor in the paths of death, and draw not nigh to them, lest you be destroyed.

4 But seek and choose for yourselves righteousness and an elect life, and walk in the paths of peace, and you shall live and prosper.

5 And hold fast my words in the thoughts of your hearts, and suffer them not to be effaced from your hearts — for I know that sinners will tempt men to evilly-entreat wisdom, so that no place may be found for her, and no manner of temptation may minish.

6 Woe to those who build unrighteousness and oppression and lay deceit as a foundation — for they shall be suddenly overthrown, and they shall have no peace.

7 Woe to those who build their houses with sin — for from all their foundations shall they be overthrown, and by the sword shall they fall, and those who acquire gold and silver in judgment suddenly shall perish.

8 Woe to you, you rich, for you have trusted in your riches, and from your riches shall you depart, because you have not remembered the Most High in the days of your riches.

9 You have committed blasphemy and unrighteousness, and have become ready for the day of slaughter, and the day of darkness and the day of the great judgment.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 94 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 94 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 94:8 — woe to you, you rich, for you have trusted in your riches and from your riches shall you depart, because you have not remembered the Most High in the days of your riches. The Epistle of Chanokh's woe-oracles against the rich and powerful directly parallel the prophetic literature of the Nevi'im — Amos 6, Yesha'yahu 5, Mikkah 2. The Book of Chanokh and the classical Hebrew prophets share the same theological framework for wealth and power: riches obtained through unrighteousness, maintained without remembrance of YHWH, and used to oppress the poor are subject to the same eschatological judgment that overtakes the kings and the mighty in the Book of Parables.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 60 The First Series of Woe Oracles — Against Oppressors

1 Oh that mine eyes were a cloud of waters that I might weep over you, and pour down my tears as a cloud of waters, that so I might rest from my trouble of heart.

2 Who has permitted you to practice reproaches and wickedness? And so judgment shall overtake you, sinners.

3 Fear not the sinners, you righteous — for again will YHWH deliver them into your hands, that you may execute judgment upon them according to your desires.

4 Woe to you who fulminate anathemas which cannot be reversed — healing shall therefore be far from you because of your sins.

5 Woe to you who requite your neighbor with evil — for you shall be requited according to your works.

6 Woe to you, lying witnesses, and to those who weigh out injustice — for suddenly shall you perish.

7 Woe to you, sinners, for you persecute the righteous — for you shall be delivered up and persecuted because of injustice, and heavy shall its yoke be upon you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 95 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 95 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 95:3 — fear not the sinners, you righteous — for YHWH will deliver them into your hands that you may execute judgment according to your desires. The Epistle addresses the righteous who are currently being persecuted by the sinners and the powerful. The word of encouragement is not passive endurance — it is the promise of reversal. The righteous will execute judgment on those who persecuted them. This is the same promise that structures the Book of Parables — the kings and the mighty who oppress the righteous will be given into the hands of the elect. The Epistle makes this promise personal and direct.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 61 The Confidence of the Righteous — The Judgment on the Rich

1 Be hopeful, you righteous, for suddenly shall the sinners perish before you, and you shall have lordship over them according to your desires.

2 And in the day of the tribulation of the sinners, your children shall mount and rise as eagles, and higher than the vultures will be your nest, and you shall ascend and enter the crevices of the earth, and the clefts of the rock forever as coneys before the unrighteous.

3 And the sirens shall rejoice over you — and the spirits of the earth shall wait and watch eagerly over you.

4 And woe unto you sinners, for your riches make you appear like the righteous, but your hearts convict you of being sinners, and this fact shall be a testimony against you for a memorial of evil deeds.

5 Woe to you who devour the finest of the wheat, and drink wine in large bowls, and tread under foot the lowly with your might.

6 Woe to you who drink water from every fountain, for suddenly shall you be consumed and wither away, because you have forsaken the fountain of life.

7 Woe to you who work unrighteousness and deceit and blasphemy — it shall be a memorial against you for evil.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 96 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 96 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 96:4 — your riches make you appear like the righteous, but your hearts convict you of being sinners. The Epistle of Chanokh identifies the specific deception of wealth: it creates an appearance of righteousness — prosperity understood as divine favor — while the interior reality is sinfulness. This is one of the most precise social-theological observations in the ancient literature. The externally prosperous sinner is the archetype of those who deny YHWH of Spirits in the Book of Parables. Wealth without remembrance of YHWH is not a sign of covenant blessing — it is, in Chanokh's analysis, a testimony of evil deeds.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 62 The Books Opened Against the Sinners — No Escape

1 Know that the angels will inquire into your doings in heaven — from the sun and from the moon and from the stars — in reference to your sins, because upon the earth you execute judgment on the righteous.

2 And witness will be summoned against you every righteous man — against you will be a witness, a sinner.

3 In those days the spirit of truth shall be established, and those who walk in it will resist the liars.

4 Woe to you who have built your houses through the labors of others, and your whole building material is the brick and stone of sin. I tell you, you shall have no peace.

5 Woe to them who reject the measure and eternal heritage of their fathers and whose souls follow after idols — for they shall have no rest.

6 Woe to those who work unrighteousness and help oppression, and slay their neighbors until the day of the great judgment.

7 For he shall cast down your glory, and bring affliction on your hearts, and shall arouse his fierce indignation and destroy you all with the sword, and all the holy and righteous shall remember your sins.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 97 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 97 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 97:1 — the angels will inquire into your doings from the sun and the moon and the stars. The heavenly bodies — whose ordered courses Chanokh documented in the Astronomical Book — are also witnesses to human conduct on earth. The same astronomical system that governs the covenant calendar is simultaneously the surveillance network of human history. Nothing done under the sun and moon and stars escapes the record. The heavenly tablets of Chapter 81 are fed by what the sun and moon and stars observe. The Astronomical Book and the Epistle are not separate literatures — they are part of the same integrated theological system.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 63 The Self-Confidence of Sinners Condemned — The Origin of Sin

1 And now I swear unto you, to the wise and to the foolish, for you shall see many things on the earth.

2 For you men shall put on more adornments than a woman, and colored garments more than a virgin — in royalty and in grandeur and in power, and in silver and in gold and in purple, and in splendor and in food they shall be poured out as water.

3 Therefore they shall be wanting in doctrine and wisdom, and they shall perish thereby together with their possessions, and with all their glory and their splendor, and in shame and in slaughter and in great destitution, their spirits shall be cast into the furnace of fire.

4 I have sworn unto you, you sinners, as a mountain has not become a slave, and a hill does not become the handmaid of a woman, even so sin has not been sent upon the earth, but man of himself has created it, and under a great curse shall they fall who commit it.

5 And barrenness has not been given to the woman, but on account of the deeds of her own hands she dies without children.

6 I have sworn unto you, you sinners, by the Holy Great One, that all your evil deeds are revealed in the heavens, and that none of your deeds of oppression are covered and hidden.

7 And think not in your spirit nor say in your heart that you do not know and that you do not see that every sin is every day recorded in heaven in the presence of the Most High.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 98 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 98 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 98:4 — sin has not been sent upon the earth, but man of himself has created it. The Epistle of Chanokh states the doctrine of human responsibility for sin with absolute clarity. Sin is not an external force imposed on humanity by the Watchers or by any other agent. Human beings created sin from within themselves. The Watchers disclosed forbidden knowledge but human beings chose to use it for evil. The cosmic explanation of the Book of the Watchers does not remove human moral accountability. The Epistle insists on both: the Watchers are responsible for what they disclosed, and humans are responsible for what they did with it.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 64 Woes Against Those Who Alter the Word — Against the Godless

1 Woe to you, you fools, for through your folly shall you perish — and you transgress against the wise, and so good hap shall not be your portion.

2 And now, know you that you are prepared for the day of destruction — wherefore do not hope to live, you sinners, but you shall depart and die, for you know no ransom — for you are prepared for the day of the great judgment, for the day of tribulation and great shame for your spirits.

3 Woe to you, you obstinate of heart, who work wickedness and eat blood — whence have you good things to eat and to drink and to be filled? From all the good things which YHWH the Most High has placed in abundance on the earth, therefore you shall have no peace.

4 Woe to you who love the deeds of unrighteousness — wherefore do you hope for good hap unto yourselves? Know that you shall be delivered into the hands of the righteous, and they shall cut off your necks and slay you, and have no mercy upon you.

5 Woe to those who write lying words and the words of error, and cause men to hear them and lead them astray, for they themselves shall have no peace but die a sudden death.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 99 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 99 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 99:10 — woe to those who write lying words and words of error, and cause men to hear them and lead them astray. Chanokh — the scribe of righteousness — pronounces woe against the scribes of unrighteousness. The same writing craft that preserves the covenant knowledge and transmits truth across generations can also be used to write lies and lead men astray. The Epistle's woe against false writing is the inverse of Chanokh's own commission. True scribal work serves YHWH's covenant. False scribal work serves the agenda of those who deny YHWH of Spirits.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 65 The Cry of the Blood of the Righteous — Angels as Witnesses

1 And in those days in one place the fathers together with their sons shall be smitten and brothers one with another shall fall in death till the streams flow with their blood.

2 For a man shall not withhold his hand from slaying his sons and his sons' sons, and the sinner shall not withhold his hand from his honored brother — from dawn till sunset they shall slay one another.

3 And the horse shall walk up to the breast in the blood of sinners, and the chariot shall be submerged to its height.

4 In those days the angels shall descend into the secret places and gather together into one place all those who brought down sin, and the Most High will arise on that day of judgment to execute great judgment among sinners.

5 And over all the righteous and holy he will appoint guardians from the holy angels to guard them as the apple of an eye, until he makes an end of all wickedness and all sin, and though the righteous sleep a long sleep, they have naught to fear.

6 And then the children of the earth shall see the wise in security, and shall understand all the words of this book, and recognize that their riches shall not be able to save them in the overthrow of their sins.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 100 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 100 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 100:5 — over all the righteous and holy he will appoint guardians from the holy angels to guard them as the apple of an eye. The same angels who reported the corruption of the earth in Chapter 9 and executed judgment in Chapter 10 are assigned as personal guardians of the righteous in the final tribulation. The apple of an eye — the most sensitive and carefully guarded part of the body — is YHWH's own image for how he keeps the covenant people: as the apple of his eye in Devarim 32:10. The Epistle's promise to the righteous is that YHWH's own care is extended through his angelic guardians to every righteous individual.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 66 The Appeal to Nature — The Sea and the Wind Obey

1 Examine the heaven, you children of heaven, and every work of the Most High, and fear you him and work no evil in his presence.

2 If he closes the windows of heaven, and withholds the rain and the dew from descending on the earth on your account, what will you do then?

3 And if he sends his anger upon you because of your deeds, you cannot petition him — for you spake proud and insolent words against his righteousness, therefore you shall have no peace.

4 And see you not the sailors of the ships, how their ships are tossed to and fro by the waves, and are shaken by the winds, and are in sore trouble?

5 And therefore do they fear because all their goodly possessions go upon the sea with them, and they have evil forebodings of heart that the sea will swallow them and they will perish therein.

6 Are not the entire sea and all its waters, and all its movements, the work of the Most High, and has he not set limits to its doings, and confined it throughout by the sand?

7 And at his reproof it is afraid and dries up, and all its fish die and all that is in it, but you sinners that are on the earth fear him not.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 101 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 101 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 101:6-7 — the entire sea and all its movements are the work of the Most High, and he has set limits to its doings and confined it throughout by the sand. And at his reproof it is afraid and dries up. The sea obeys YHWH's reproof. The same sea whose boundaries are set by the cosmic oath of Chapter 69 responds to YHWH's voice with fear. The natural creation is obedient. Human sinners are not. The Epistle uses the sea's obedience — and the terror of sailors — as an argument for human fear of YHWH. If the sea itself fears YHWH's reproof, on what basis do sinners live without fear?

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 67 The Comfort of the Righteous Dead — The Accusation of the Wicked

1 In those days if he brings a grievous fire upon you, where will you flee, and where will you find deliverance? And when he launches forth his word against you, will you not be affrighted and fear?

2 And all the luminaries shall be affrighted with great fear, and all the earth shall be affrighted and tremble and be alarmed.

3 And all the angels shall execute their commands and shall seek to hide themselves from the presence of the Great Glory, and the children of earth shall tremble and quake — and you sinners shall be cursed forever, and you shall have no peace.

4 Fear not, you souls of the righteous, and be hopeful you that have died in righteousness.

5 And grieve not if your soul into Sheol has descended in grief, and that in your life your body fared not according to your goodness, but wait for the day of the judgment of sinners and for the day of cursing and chastisement.

6 And yet when you die the sinners speak over you: As we die, so die the righteous, and what benefit do they reap for their deeds?

7 Behold, even as we, so do they die in grief and darkness, and what have they more than we? From henceforth we are equal. And what will they receive and what will they see forever? Behold, they too have died, and henceforth and forever shall they see no light.

8 I tell you, you sinners, you are content to eat and drink, and rob and sin and strip men naked, and acquire wealth and see good days.

9 Have you seen the righteous how their end falls out, that no manner of violence is found in them till their death?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 102 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 102 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 102:6-7 — the accusation of the wicked: as we die, so die the righteous — what benefit do they reap for their deeds? From henceforth we are equal. The philosophical objection to covenant faithfulness — that death equalizes the righteous and the wicked, making righteousness pointless — is stated here in the Epistle as the voice of the sinners standing over the graves of the righteous. This is the same question wrestled with in Iyov, in Tehillim 73, and in Kohelet. The Epistle's answer is the Book of Parables' entire argument: the righteous are not equal to the wicked at death. The holding places of Chapter 22 keep them separate. The great judgment will reveal the difference that death appeared to erase.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 68 The Lot of the Righteous — Written in the Heavenly Books

1 Now, therefore, I swear to you, the righteous, by the glory of the Great and Honored and Mighty One in dominion, and by his greatness I swear to you.

2 I know a mystery and have read the heavenly tablets, and have seen the holy books, and have found written therein and inscribed regarding them.

3 That all goodness and joy and glory are prepared for them, and written down for the spirits of those who have died in righteousness, and that manifold good shall be given to you in recompense for your labors, and that your lot is abundantly beyond the lot of the living.

4 And the spirits of you who have died in righteousness shall live and rejoice, and their spirits shall not perish, nor their memorial from before the face of the Great One unto all the generations of the world — wherefore fear not their contumely.

5 Woe to you, you sinners, when you have died, if you die in the wealth of your sins, and those who are like you say regarding you: Blessed are the sinners, they have seen all their days.

6 And how have they died in prosperity and in wealth, and have not seen tribulation or murder in their life, and they have died in honor, and judgment has not been executed on them during their life.

7 Know you, that their souls will be made to descend into Sheol and they shall be wretched in their great tribulation.

8 And into darkness and chains and a burning flame where there is grievous judgment shall your spirits enter, and the great judgment shall be for all the generations of the world.

9 Woe to you, for you shall have no peace.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 103 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 103 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 103:2-3 — I have read the heavenly tablets and found written therein that all goodness and joy and glory are prepared for the spirits of those who died in righteousness, and that manifold good shall be given in recompense for your labors. Chanokh's authority for this promise is direct: he read the heavenly tablets. The same tablets that recorded all human deeds in Chapter 81 contain the prepared inheritance of the righteous dead. The covenant justice that the sinners believe death has cancelled is in fact preserved in written form in heaven. Chanokh has seen it written. The promise is not theological inference — it is reported testimony from a witness who read the record himself.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 69 The Command to Write — The Final Warning to the Elect

1 I swear unto you, that in heaven the angels remember you for good before the glory of the Great One, and your names are written before the glory of the Great One.

2 Be hopeful — for aforetime you were put to shame through ill and affliction, but now you shall shine as the lights of heaven, you shall shine and you shall be seen, and the portals of heaven shall be opened to you.

3 And in your cry, cry for judgment, and it shall appear to you — for all your tribulation shall be visited on the rulers, and on all who helped those who plundered you.

4 Be hopeful, and cast not away your hopes for you shall have great joy as the angels of heaven.

5 What shall you be obliged to do? You shall not have to hide on the day of the great judgment and you shall not be found as sinners, and the eternal judgment shall be far from you for all the generations of the world.

6 And now I know this mystery, that sinners will alter and pervert the words of righteousness in many ways, and will speak wicked words, and lie, and practice great deceits, and write books concerning their words.

7 But when they write down truthfully all my words in their languages, and do not change or minish ought from my words but write them all down truthfully — all that I first testified concerning them —

8 Then, I know another mystery, that books will be given to the righteous and the wise to become a cause of joy and uprightness and much wisdom.

9 And to them shall the books be given, and they shall believe in them and rejoice over them, and then shall all the righteous who have learnt therefrom all the paths of uprightness be recompensed.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 104 in Aramaic. Verse 104:9-10 — the warning about altering the words of righteousness — is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 104 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 104:9-11 — sinners will alter and pervert the words of righteousness in many ways and write books concerning their words. But those who write down all his words truthfully, all that I first testified — books will be given to the righteous and wise to become a cause of joy and wisdom. The Book of Chanokh anticipates its own corruption and correction. Chanokh predicts that false scribes will alter the words of righteousness — exactly what the Epistle warned against in verse 99:10. But he also predicts that truthful transmission will produce books given to the righteous as a source of wisdom and joy. The Qumran community understood themselves as the righteous scribes transmitting Chanokh's words faithfully, in contrast to those who had perturbed or excluded them.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 70 The Son of Man Shall Be a Staff — Another Brief Word

1 In those days the Lord bade them to summon and testify to the children of earth concerning their wisdom — show it to them, for you are their guides, and a recompense over the whole earth.

2 For I and my son will be united with them forever in the paths of uprightness in their lives, and you shall have peace — rejoice, you children of uprightness. Amen.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves a portion of Chapter 105.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 105 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 105:2 — I and my son will be united with them forever in the paths of uprightness. YHWH speaks in the first person here — I and my son. The identity of the son is the same question that structures the entire Book of Parables. Whether son refers to the Elect One, to Chanokh in his translated state, or to the covenant people as YHWH's sons in the sense of Devarim 14:1 — the text preserves the phrase as stated. The Orit Ge'ez makes no effort to clarify the ambiguity. The ancient record holds it open.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 71 The Birth of Noakh — His Radiant Appearance

1 And after some days my son Metushelakh took a wife for his son Lamekh, and she became pregnant by him and bore a son.

2 And his body was white as snow and red as the blooming of a rose, and the hair of his head and his long locks were white as wool, and his eyes beautiful. And when he opened his eyes, he lighted up the whole house like the sun, and the whole house was very bright.

3 And thereupon he arose in the hands of the midwife, opened his mouth, and conversed with YHWH of righteousness.

4 And his father Lamekh was afraid of him and fled, and came to his father Metushelakh.

5 And he said unto him: I have begotten a strange son, diverse from and unlike man, and resembling the sons of the Creator of heaven, and his nature is different and he is not like us, and his eyes are as the rays of the sun, and his countenance is glorious.

6 And it seems to me that he is not sprung from me but from the angels, and I fear that in his days a wonder may be wrought on the earth.

7 And now, my father, I am here to petition you and implore you that you may go to Chanokh, our father, and learn from him the truth, for his dwelling place is amongst the angels.

8 And he went to Chanokh and said: Chanokh, my father, tell me what this means — for a son has been born to my son Lamekh, unlike the other children, and his nature is not like man's nature, and the color of his body is whiter than snow and redder than the bloom of a rose, and the hair of his head is whiter than white wool, and his eyes are like the rays of the sun, and he opened his eyes and thereupon lighted up the whole house.

9 And the name of this child shall be Noakh, for he shall leave to you a remnant, and he and his sons shall be saved from the destruction.

10 His father and his son Metushelakh rejoiced and were glad, and the seed of life shall be planted upon the whole earth for those who are saved from the Flood, and this plant shall be preserved on the earth, and it shall not be destroyed from that day forevermore.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 106 in Aramaic. The birth of Noakh narrative is also preserved in the Genesis Apocryphon (1QapGen) from Cave 1, which provides an Aramaic expansion of the Noakh birth story in the first person voice of Lamekh. The two texts confirm each other and reflect a shared tradition about Noakh's remarkable appearance at birth.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 106 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 106:2 — his body was white as snow and red as the blooming of a rose, and his hair was white as wool, and his eyes beautiful. When he opened his eyes he lighted up the whole house like the sun. Noakh's physical appearance at birth — radiant white, illuminating the house with his opened eyes — is described in terms that parallel the appearance of the Great Glory on his throne in Chapter 14: his raiment shone more brightly than the sun and was whiter than any snow. Noakh carries a physical imprint of divine light. Lamekh fears he was sired by angels because of this appearance. Chanokh confirms that Noakh is indeed Lamekh's son — but that he is set apart for a specific covenant purpose: the preservation of life through the Flood.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 72 Chanokh's Final Word on the Flood and the Remnant

1 Then I answered and said to him: YHWH will do a new thing on the earth, and this I have already seen in a vision, and make known to you that in the generation of my father Yered some of the angels of heaven transgressed the word of YHWH.

2 And behold they commit sin and transgress the law, and have united themselves with women and commit sin with them, and have married some of them, and have begotten children by them.

3 And there shall be a great destruction over the whole earth, and there shall be a deluge and there shall be a great destruction for one year.

4 And this son who has been born unto you shall be left on the earth, and his three children shall be saved with him when all mankind that are on the earth shall die.

5 And he and his sons shall be saved, and when all mankind who are on the earth shall die, he shall be saved.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 107 in Aramaic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 107 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 107:1 — YHWH will do a new thing on the earth, and this I have already seen in a vision. Chanokh's response to Metushelakh about the birth of Noakh draws on his own visions — the first dream vision of Chapter 83 — to confirm Lamekh's son's covenant significance. Everything in the Book of Chanokh is integrated: the visions of the Dream Visions section are the same events referenced in the Epistle. The Astronomical Book's calendar governs the timing of the Flood. The Book of the Watchers' account of the Watchers' transgression is the cause that Chanokh now explains to Metushelakh as the reason for the coming destruction. The book is one unified revelation across all its sections.

CHANOKH — CHAPTER 73 The Final Chapter — The Reward of the Righteous — The Destiny of the Wicked

1 Another book which Chanokh wrote for his son Metushelakh and for those who will come after him, and keep the law in the last days.

2 You who have done good shall wait for those days till an end is made of those who work evil, and an end of the might of the transgressors.

3 And wait you indeed till sin has passed away, for their names shall be blotted out of the book of life and out of the holy books, and their seed shall be destroyed forever, and their spirits shall be slain, and they shall cry and make lamentation in a place that is a chaotic wilderness, and in the fire shall they burn, for there is no earth there.

4 And I saw there something like an invisible cloud, for by reason of its depth I could not look over it, and I saw a flame of fire blazing brightly, and things like shining mountains circling and sweeping to and fro.

5 And I asked one of the holy angels who was with me and said unto him: What is this shining thing? For it is not a heaven but only the flame of a blazing fire, and the voice of weeping and crying and lamentation and strong pain.

6 And he said unto me: This place which you see — here are cast the spirits of sinners and blasphemers, and of those who work wickedness, and of those who pervert everything that YHWH has spoken through the mouth of the prophets — even the things that shall be.

7 For some of them are written and inscribed above in the heaven, in order that the angels may read them and know that which shall befall the sinners, and the spirits of the humble, and of those who have afflicted their bodies, and been recompensed by YHWH — and of those who have been put to shame by wicked men.

8 Who love YHWH and loved neither gold nor silver nor any of the good things which are in the world, but gave over their bodies to torture.

9 Who, since they came into being, longed not after earthly food, but regarded everything as a passing breath, and lived accordingly, and YHWH tried them much, and their spirits were found pure so that they should bless his name.

10 And all the blessings destined for them I have recounted in the books. And he has assigned them their recompense, because they have been found to be such as loved heaven more than their life in the world, and though they were trodden under foot of wicked men, and experienced abuse and reviling from them and were put to shame, yet they blessed me.

11 And now I will summon the spirits of the good who belong to the generation of light, and I will transform those who were born in darkness, who in the flesh were not recompensed with such honor as their faithfulness deserved.

12 And I will bring forth in shining light those who have loved my holy name, and I will seat each on the throne of his honor.

13 And they shall be resplendent for times without number, for righteousness is the judgment of YHWH, for to the faithful he will give faithfulness in the habitation of upright paths.

14 And they shall see those who were born in darkness led into darkness, while the righteous shall be resplendent.

15 And the sinners shall cry aloud and see them resplendent, and they indeed shall go where days and seasons are prescribed for them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEng (4Q212) preserves portions of Chapter 108 in Aramaic. Chapter 108 is the final chapter of the Book of Chanokh and is confirmed in the Cave 4 manuscripts. The conclusion of the Epistle of Chanokh — and of the entire Book of Chanokh — is attested in the DSS Aramaic fragments.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 108 in full, completing the entire Book of Chanokh — all 108 chapters.

[MASORETIC VARIANT]

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 108:8-10 — who loved YHWH and loved neither gold nor silver nor any of the good things in the world, but gave over their bodies to torture. Who longed not after earthly food but regarded everything as a passing breath. And YHWH tried them much, and their spirits were found pure, so that they should bless his name. The final description of the righteous in the Book of Chanokh is the portrait of those who chose the covenant and its costs over the world's comfort and its rewards. They were trodden under foot, abused, reviled, shamed — and they blessed YHWH. Their spirits were found pure in the trial.

THE BOOK OF CHANOKH — COMPLETE — 73 CHAPTERS

Volume Six — The Ancient Covenant Record

I WILL BRING FORTH IN SHINING LIGHT THOSE WHO HAVE LOVED MY HOLY NAME

AND I WILL SEAT EACH ON THE THRONE OF HIS HONOR

Chanokh Chapter 73 — Verse 12
